notes: O game funciona apenas no menu principal. Quando o ponteiro do mouse é movimentando, o FPS do jogo cai drasticamente. Nada disso acontecia na versão anterior do Proton, que rodava o jogo perfeitamente.
title: Tomb Raider: Anniversary

notes:
[PT-BR]
Eu não sei porque, mas após a vinda da nova versão do Proton (4.0), eu tive que baixar e instalar o jogo de novo, e pior, meu save foi corrompido (ele ainda está lá, mas quando eu boto pra continuar o jogo, ele fecha no meio do carregamento) então tive que criar um novo. Mais uma coisa: ao movimentar o mouse, o FPS do game cai drasticamente.

[ENGLISH]
I do not know why, but after new version of Proton (4.0) came in, I had to download and install the game again, and worse, my save was corrupted (it's still there, but when I resume the game it closes in the middle of loading) so I had to create a new one. One more thing: by moving the mouse (you know, the hardware in your hand), the game's FPS drops dramatically.
title: Tomb Raider: Anniversary

notes: Runs without any issues.  Using wired Xbox 360 controller, but onscreen button prompts are still for keyboard and mouse.  Possibly fixed with in game settings
title: Tomb Raider: Anniversary

notes: Game works on Proton out of the box, but mouse movements are either uncontrollable or they create massive frame drops. Workaround is to use a controller. I installed manually D9VK, but it showed only a black screen.
title: Tomb Raider: Anniversary

notes:
There's 2 glaring issues with this. 

1. Brightness & Contrast doesn't change anything, which means the game can be too bright/dark or too washed out/too vibrant.

2. Awful mouse support. It's unplayable when playing mouse and keyboard. It's like the mouse stops working every second and then restarts again. You can't move the camera or even the in game cursor without insane lag. Complete let down on an otherwise great game. 

If played with a controller, the game runs perfectly so far with no glitches.
title: Tomb Raider: Anniversary

notes: I noticed horrible mouse stuttering. The only way to fix it was to run at native desktop resolution and to disable v-sync. Otherwise the game runs very well.
title: Tomb Raider: Anniversary

notes: My mouse issue has been 99% fixed, it's got a slight twitch that doesn't happen in Windows but it's not that noticeable. The only thing that doesn't work is the Brightness/Contrast bars.
title: Tomb Raider: Anniversary

notes:
This would be borderline Platinum but there's a major issue with this game. The mouse constantly freezes every 2 seconds. 4.2.4 it was even worse, 4.2.5 has slightly fixed it and at times will perform normally and it's playable but most times it's horribly laggy which freezes the game. Controller works 100% fine and causes no issues, if you play with the controller it's Platinum. 

Also, the Brightness and Contrast meters don't actually do anything in Linux, when they do in Windows. Other games under Proton can change the brightness/contrast fine so it's just this game and Tomb Raider Legend + Underworld.
title: Tomb Raider: Anniversary

notes: fps drops occasionally, otherwise great
